executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 301,811.98 inc VAT (274,726.07 ex VAT) for the week ending 20 June 2026.
Overall margin finished at 17.79%, which requires close monitoring given the mixed departmental performance across the store.
Total waste for the week was 3,104.21, representing 1.13% of sales, with Dairy (769.73), Provisions (713.93), and Deli (481.83) as the three largest waste contributors.

Fresh departments are showing significant anomalies in reported cost of sales, with Poultry (-581.42 COS) and Pork (-185.70 COS) both recording negative cost of sales figures, indicating stock count or purchase data issues that require urgent investigation.
Cigarettes and Deli are both reporting 100% margin in the P&L, which is not reflective of true trading and points to missing purchases or COS data for both departments.
Produce remains the highest-turnover fresh department at 26,297.16 in sales but is carrying a COS of 8,152.47 with no closing stock recorded, which inflates cost and understates true margin.

The Fresh and Chilled division collectively accounted for the majority of departmental sales at 90,825.93, yet the integrity of the underlying stock data must be addressed before accurate profitability conclusions can be drawn.
biggest_wins: Beef generated 11,017.23 gross profit on sales of 11,743.72, achieving a reported P&L margin of 93.81% against a scan margin of 17.54%; while the P&L margin is distorted by stock movement, Beef is trading strongly with well-managed stock levels moving from 7,116.87 opening to 6,390.38 closing.

Produce delivered the highest absolute gross profit contribution in the fresh division at 18,144.69 on sales of 26,297.16, representing a solid volume performance, though the absence of a closing stock figure needs to be resolved to confirm true margin.

Lamb achieved a reported P&L margin of 79.76% on sales of 1,037.45 with a scan margin of 23.78%, and waste was not flagged as a concern in this department, suggesting good stock rotation and ordering discipline.

Fish produced a gross profit of 2,229.23 on sales of 2,271.51, with COS of only 42.28, reflecting tight stock management; however, waste of 310.35 equates to approximately 13.7% of Fish sales and should be addressed.

Provisions posted near-100% P&L margin with profit of 17,997.51 on sales of 18,004.60 and a minimal COS of 7.09, indicating very low stock held; scan margin of 21.76% is the benchmark to work back towards once purchase data is fully captured.
biggest_risks: Poultry is reporting a negative COS of -581.42 on sales of 8,092.91, producing an artificial margin of 107.18%; this is not a genuine trading result and is almost certainly caused by a closing stock count that exceeds opening stock plus unrecorded purchases - this must be investigated and corrected immediately.

Pork similarly shows a negative COS of -185.70 on sales of 1,128.82, yielding a reported margin of 116.45%; as with Poultry, this figure is not credible and likely reflects a stock count error or missing purchase data.

Cigarettes is showing a 100% margin with no purchases, opening stock, closing stock, or COS recorded; this is a data integrity failure - Cigarettes purchases must be entered and reconciled against scan margin of 9.39% to establish true profitability.

Deli is similarly reporting 100% margin with no COS data; with a scan margin of 71.71% and waste of 481.83, the department almost certainly has significant unrecorded cost. Deli waste alone at 481.83 needs to be reviewed against production volumes.

Dairy waste of 769.73 is the single largest waste figure across all departments this week. With no standalone Dairy P&L line visible in departmental reporting, this cost is being absorbed elsewhere and must be tracked to its correct department to understand true margin impact.

Provisions waste of 713.93 is the second highest waste category and is disproportionate for a department that carries minimal stock (opening 24.18, closing 17.09). This level of Provisions waste should be investigated for mis-categorisation or ordering issues.

Produce has no closing stock recorded, meaning the full opening stock value of 8,152.47 has been expensed as COS; this understates Produce margin and overstates cost. A closing count must be completed and entered to give an accurate Produce GP figure.

Bacon and Loose SRP delivered the weakest margin of any department with data, at 61.55% P&L margin against a scan margin of 11.99%; while stock movement accounts for some of this, the department warrants a review of pricing, waste, and shrinkage.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: No COS, purchases, opening or closing stock data present. P&L shows 100% margin which is incorrect. Scan margin is 9.39%. Purchase data must be entered urgently to produce a valid margin figure.

Beef: Strong trading week with sales of 11,743.72 and gross profit of 11,017.23. Stock reduced from 7,116.87 to 6,390.38. Scan margin of 17.54% is the true benchmark and should be reconciled against P&L once purchases are confirmed.

Lamb: Sales of 1,037.45 with COS of 210.02 and gross profit of 827.43. Reported margin of 79.76% reflects stock drawdown. Scan margin of 23.78% is the working margin target. No significant waste flagged.

Pork: Negative COS of -185.70 on sales of 1,128.82 is not a valid result. Closing stock of 669.78 exceeds opening stock of 484.08 without purchases recorded, which is driving the distortion. Waste of 14.70 is low. Stock count and purchases must be reviewed.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Margin of 61.55% on sales of 1,082.51 is the weakest performing department with a P&L result. Stock fell from 1,174.44 to 758.17 with COS of 416.27. Scan margin of 11.99% suggests true margin is significantly lower. A full review of this category is recommended.

Poultry: Negative COS of -581.42 on sales of 8,092.91 is not credible. Closing stock of 3,336.37 is significantly higher than opening stock of 2,754.95 with no purchases recorded, creating the anomaly. Waste of 191.24 is also present. Data must be corrected before any margin assessment is possible.

Fish: Sales of 2,271.51 with COS of 42.28 and gross profit of 2,229.23. However, waste of 310.35 is high relative to sales, equating to approximately 13.7% of Fish sales. Stock management and ordering frequency should be reviewed to reduce waste exposure.

Produce: Highest fresh department sales at 26,297.16 with gross profit of 18,144.69. No closing stock recorded, causing full opening stock of 8,152.47 to be expensed. Waste of 730.02 is the largest fresh waste figure. Closing count must be submitted and waste reduction reviewed.

Deli: Sales of 10,599.52 with no COS data, producing a 100% margin. Scan margin of 71.71% reflects the true trading margin. Waste of 481.83 is notable and production planning should be reviewed. COS data must be corrected.

Provisions: Sales of 18,004.60 with COS of 7.09 and gross profit of 17,997.51. Scan margin of 21.76% is the relevant benchmark. Waste of 713.93 is very high for this department and is the second highest waste category store-wide - root cause investigation required.
action_plan: Investigate and correct the negative COS figures for Poultry (-581.42) and Pork (-185.70) immediately - review closing stock counts and confirm whether purchase invoices have been posted for the week.
Enter all outstanding purchase data for Cigarettes and reconcile P&L margin against scan margin of 9.39% before end of the following trading day.
Submit a closing stock count for Deli and ensure all production costs and purchases are entered so that a valid COS and margin can be established for the week.

Complete and submit a closing stock figure for Produce to correct the COS distortion caused by the full opening stock of 8,152.47 being expensed; escalate to department manager to ensure this is a standard end-of-week procedure going forward.

Investigate Provisions waste of 713.93 as a priority - identify which product lines are generating this waste, review ordering levels, and determine whether any waste has been mis-coded to this department.

Review Dairy waste of 769.73 with the relevant department manager; assess date code management, ordering patterns, and whether promotional activity or reduced shelf life product is being managed effectively.

Conduct a Fish ordering and waste review with the department manager; waste of 310.35 against sales of 2,271.51 represents approximately 13.7% of Fish sales and is commercially unsustainable without corrective action.

Review Deli waste of 481.83 against production records; implement or reinforce daily production planning based on recent sales patterns and reduce end-of-day waste through markdowns or portion size adjustments.

Review Produce waste of 730.02 against sales of 26,297.16; while proportionally lower than Fish, the absolute figure is the highest in the fresh division and should be addressed through improved date code rotation and ordering discipline.

Request a full Bacon and Loose SRP category review, including pricing, ordering, and shrinkage, given the weakest recorded margin of 61.55% against a scan margin of 11.99%; assess whether promotional activity or stock losses are contributing.

Ensure all departmental stock counts are completed and submitted by the Saturday close each week without exception, as missing closing stock data for Produce and missing purchase data across multiple departments is preventing accurate weekly P&L reporting.

Schedule a department manager briefing early in the following week to present waste figures by category, set waste reduction targets, and assign ownership for corrective actions in Dairy, Provisions, Deli, and Fish.
